title: Design System: Recursive Coding Agents Talk

display.purpose: Stage-readable slide titles and single-claim openings.

headline.purpose: Section turns and explanatory slide headings.

motion.name: slide-rise
motion.purpose: Native scroll-driven content entrance with visible resting fallback.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Borders, backgrounds, and subtle hover transitions.

components.name: Deck CTA
components.description: Primary external link used at the end of the deck.
components.css: .ds-deck-cta { display: inline-block; margin-top: 1.6rem; padding: 0.85rem 1.8rem; border-radius: 999px; background: var(--deck-accent, oklch(0.3012 0 0)); color: var(--deck-accent-ink, oklch(0.9169 0.0175 99.6160)); font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 1.1rem; font-weight: 700; text-decoration: none; box-shadow: 0 10px 30px -12px color-mix(in srgb, var(--deck-accent, #333) 50%, transparent); transition: transform 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease, filter 0.18s ease; } .ds-deck-cta:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); filter: brightness(1.05); box-shadow: 0 16px 40px -14px color-mix(in srgb, var(--deck-accent, #333) 65%, transparent); } .ds-deck-cta:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--deck-accent, oklch(0.3012 0 0)); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Evidence Preview
components.description: Linked article or paper artifact with image-led proof.
components.html: RAW.worksCode Execution As Reasoning
components.css: .ds-evidence-preview { display: block; overflow: hidden; padding: 1rem; border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--deck-text, oklch(0.235 0 0)) 16%, transparent); border-radius: 8px; background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--deck-text, oklch(0.235 0 0)) 4%, var(--deck-bg, oklch(0.9195 0.0169 88.0030))); color: var(--deck-text, oklch(0.235 0 0)); text-decoration: none; box-shadow: 0 18px 50px -34px color-mix(in oklch, var(--deck-text, oklch(0.235 0 0)) 48%, transparent); transition: border-color 0.16s ease, background 0.16s ease, transform 0.16s ease; } .ds-evidence-preview img { display: block; width: 100%; height: 160px; object-fit: cover; object-position: top center; border-radius: 5px; border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--deck-text, oklch(0.235 0 0)) 12%, transparent); background: white; } .ds-evidence-preview:hover { border-color: color-mix(in oklch, var(--deck-accent, oklch(0.3012 0 0)) 48%, var(--deck-text, oklch(0.235 0 0))); transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-evidence-preview:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--deck-accent, oklch(0.3012 0 0)); outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-evidence-caption { display: grid; gap: 0.35rem; margin-top: 0.75rem; } .ds-evidence-source { color: var(--deck-accent, oklch(0.3012 0 0)); font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.13em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-evidence-preview strong { font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1.14; }

components.name: Rubric Row
components.description: Highlighted row for the RLM rubric table.
components.html: Recursive Language Modelpasses every gate✓
components.css: .ds-rubric-row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) auto; gap: 0.4rem 1rem; align-items: center; padding: 1rem; border-block: 2px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary, oklch(0.3012 0 0)) 50%, transparent); background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary, oklch(0.3012 0 0)) 10%, transparent); color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.235 0 0)); font-family: Inter, sans-serif; } .ds-rubric-row strong { color: var(--primary, oklch(0.3012 0 0)); font-size: 0.95rem; } .ds-rubric-row span { color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.4688 0.0136 84.5932)); font-size: 0.78rem; } .ds-rubric-check { grid-row: 1 / span 2; grid-column: 2; color: var(--primary, oklch(0.3012 0 0)); font-size: 1.2rem; font-weight: 800; }

narrative.overview:
The deck should feel like a precise briefing from someone who has built the thing being argued for. It is public-facing and persuasive, but the persuasion comes from evidence, structure, and restraint rather than decoration. The current system uses a warm stage background, near-black ink, large Inter headlines, real artifact previews, and small interaction details to keep the argument grounded.

The personality is rigorous, pragmatic, and provocative. A slide can make a sharp claim, but the next visual beat should give the viewer a citation, a rubric, a repository, or a concrete proof path. The system explicitly rejects generic AI SaaS gradients, template card grids, academic wall-of-text slides, and hacker-terminal cosplay.
keyCharacteristics: High-contrast, stage-readable typography with one dominant idea per viewport.
Warm neutral surfaces with near-black type and sparing accent use.
Evidence imagery: article cards, paper previews, repository cards, and linked artifacts.
Responsive, scroll-snapped slides that remain a normal website with anchors.
Minimal motion that supports navigation and does not gate content.

rules.name: The Evidence Before Color Rule
rules.body: Color should never be the main proof of importance. If a claim matters, support it with hierarchy, structure, and a real artifact before adding chroma.

rules.name: The No AI Glow Rule
rules.body: Do not add generic purple-blue AI gradients, neon glows, or blurred decorative blobs. This talk is about reliability, not atmosphere.

rules.name: The One Thought Rule
rules.body: If a slide needs multiple display-scale thoughts, split the slide. The deck should advance in clean turns.

rules.name: The No Wall Rule
rules.body: Do not solve weak hierarchy by adding more text. Reduce, separate, or turn the evidence into a visual artifact.

rules.name: The Artifact Lift Rule
rules.body: Shadows are allowed when the object is a real artifact to inspect: a paper preview, article preview, repository card, or CTA. Do not apply elevation to every list item.

dos: Do keep each slide focused on one claim or narrative turn.
Do support provocation with visible evidence: papers, article cards, repository previews, rubrics, or links.
Do preserve responsive slide layouts so the deck works as a real website, not a scaled fixed canvas.
Do use real artifact imagery when a slide cites external work.
Do keep muted text dark enough for WCAG AA contrast.
donts: Don't use generic AI SaaS gradients.
Don't build template card grids where every card has the same icon-heading-body pattern.
Don't use hacker-terminal cosplay or monospace as shorthand for technical credibility.
Don't add colored side-stripe borders, gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, or blurred orb backgrounds.
Don't add an eyebrow automatically to every new slide; labels must earn their place as orientation.
